mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real GDP growth indicate a supply-demand mismatch where weakening output fails to offset collapsing demand, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 300, a 25-basis-point cut in the federal funds rate acts as a credibility signal that the central bank has successfully anchored expectations, triggering a positive re-rating of equity multiples and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS decreases by 0.25%
failure_condition: Equity multiples fail to expand following rate cuts when inflation remains elevated, or forward returns are indistinguishable from baseline during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects resilient consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or zero correlation with forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows, or housing starts revisions consistently reverse the initial price impact.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, it confirms a labor market inflection point that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 100000 for two months and UNRATE rises above 4.0%
failure_condition: The joint labor deterioration signal fails to predict earnings revisions or forward returns, or the signal triggers during periods of structural labor force participation shifts rather than cyclical downturns.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained environment where industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth, capacity utilization tightens, allowing firms to pass through higher costs and sustain margins, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate exceeds GDPC1 growth rate
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when supply growth exceeds demand growth, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of global supply chain normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Rising commercial and industrial loan balances indicate expanding corporate leverage and investment activity, which historically precedes earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline market model, or rising credit balances consistently precede equity drawdowns due to debt sustainability concerns.
